symbols: arrows in Sebastian's body — the instruments of his attempted martyrdom
Irene tending his wounds — Christian compassion and the ministry of healing
cherub offering a crown — the martyr's crown of glory awaiting the faithful
Sebastian bound but alive — the resilience of faith that cannot be destroyed by persecution
description: A dramatic Baroque composition showing a semi-nude male figure (Sebastian) lying wounded with his arms raised and bound, arrows visible in his body. A young woman in a red robe (Irene) kneels beside him, tending to his wounds with cloth. An older woman watches from the dark background. Above, a cherub descends holding what appears to be a laurel crown or palm. The lighting is dramatic with strong chiaroscuro, the illuminated figures of Sebastian and Irene emerging from deep darkness. The composition is intimate and compassionate.
